_comment: Pronunciation overrides applied to narration scripts before TTS ONLY (committed transcripts keep the real words). Matched case-sensitively on word boundaries, longest-first. Initialisms are spelled as phonetic syllables joined by HYPHENS (e.g. API -> 'eigh-pee-eye') so Kokoro reads them as one connected word; SPACES make it read staccato, distinct letters. A lone capital letter is read as a word (e.g. 'A' -> the article 'uh'), not its letter-name, hence the spellings. Letter-sound guide for Kokoro: A='eigh' (/eɪ/, as in eight), I='eye', E='ee'; 'ay' voices as /aɪ/ so use it only for the letter I. Some acronyms read fine raw and need no entry (e.g. SDET). Real-word overrides (JSON -> Jason) stay as-is. Extend freely. See docs/audio-pipeline.md.
